dailyJudgementSnippet: OpenAI narrowly had the best day on HN: Codex was the only tracked product that got clear user praise, while Anthropic drew heavy backlash over Claude Code quota/cache behavior and Google Gemini got a pointed quota bait-and-switch complaint [E2][E1][E4][E3].
winnerExplanation: OpenAI wins because the strongest positive signal of the day was favorable comparison-shopping toward Codex, which commenters described as more generous and more accurate for some work than Claude Code [E2]. Anthropic had much more coverage, but it was overwhelmingly negative across two separate front-page stories about quota exhaustion, hidden cache-TTL changes, regressions, and worsening UX [E1][E4]. Google Gemini only showed up in a negative complaint about quota expectations [E3], and Microsoft Copilot had no relevant signal.

openai.judgementSnippet: Mixed but net positive: Codex benefited from direct favorable comparisons against Claude Code, though ChatGPT also picked up a smaller negative aside about being "watered down" [E2][E5].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Strongly negative: two separate front-page threads focused on Claude Code quota drain, cache/TTL downgrades, regressions, and declining trust in Anthropic's product behavior [E1][E4].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Negative: the only clear Gemini signal was a complaint about quota bait-and-switch and poor treatment of paying users [E3].

evidence.summary: Front-page story 'Pro Max 5x quota exhausted in 1.5 hours despite moderate usage' centered on Claude Code. Top comments reported long exploration loops, rapid quota drain, session-limit pain, and fear around issue closures; an Anthropic team member acknowledged prompt-cache and agent/plugin behaviors as active investigation areas.

In the Claude Code quota thread, commenters said they bought a Codex plan because it felt more generous with usage and more accurate for some backend/debugging tasks, and another user said they had moved to Codex for CLI use and were happy with the setup.

A top-level comment in the quota-exhaustion thread said the Google Gemini/Antigravity community had recently faced quota bait-and-switch behavior and contempt from Google, prompting the commenter to move to other tools.

Front-page story 'Anthropic downgraded cache TTL on March 6th' drew comments about hidden product weakening, worse quota economics, more bugs, poorer model performance, and a general spread of negative sentiment around Claude/Claude Code.
Within the Anthropic TTL discussion, one commenter added that 'ChatGPT is also being watered down,' giving OpenAI a smaller, secondary negative mention.
